primitives.name: Element Registry
primitives.description: Register and query UI elements by semantic ID. The foundation primitive that all others depend on. Scans the UI tree (DOM, widget tree, view hierarchy) for annotated elements and maintains a live registry keyed by probe ID.

methods.name: query
methods.description: Look up a single element by its semantic probe ID. Returns the full 15-attribute ProbeElement or null if not found.
methods.implementation_notes: Web: Map<string, ProbeElement> lookup in ElementRegistry. Flutter: ProbeBinding.query(). iOS: ProbeRegistry singleton scan. Android: SemanticsNode tree walk. Windows: IProbeRegistry.Query(). RN: self-registration registry.

methods.name: queryAll
methods.description: Return all registered elements, optionally filtered by ProbeType (e.g. only 'data-container' or 'action' elements).

methods.name: queryPage
methods.description: Return a summary of the current page: its ID, state, all elements, and which elements are still loading/submitting (unready). If no PAGE element found, derives state from unready elements count.
methods.return_type: { id: string; state: string; elements: ProbeElement[]; unreadyElements: string[] }

primitives.name: State Exposure
primitives.description: Expose real-time element state (loaded/error/disabled/submitting/etc.) as structured data instead of requiring screenshot interpretation. State includes current value, previous value, timestamp, open/closed for modals, and validation errors.

methods.name: getState
methods.description: Get the current state object for a specific element. Throws Error (not ProbeActionError) if element not found.
methods.implementation_notes: Web: sdk/web/src/probe.ts — throws Error('Element not found: id') if registry miss

methods.name: getStates
methods.description: Batch query: get state objects for multiple elements at once. Silently skips IDs not found.

primitives.name: Event Stream
primitives.description: Subscribe to state changes and events using an event-driven model instead of polling or fixed timeouts. Provides promise-based waitFor methods for test synchronization and callback-based subscriptions for continuous monitoring.

methods.name: waitForState
methods.signature: (id: string, state: string, timeout?: number) => Promise<void>
methods.description: Wait until a specific element reaches the desired state string. Resolves immediately if already in that state. Rejects on timeout (default 10s). Implementation: MutationObserver on data-probe-state attribute (web), frame callback diff (Flutter), polling loop (iOS/Android).

methods.name: waitForPageReady
methods.description: Wait until the page is ready: either the PAGE element reaches 'loaded' state, or all elements leave loading/submitting states. Default timeout 30s. Web implementation is fully event-driven (re-polls on state-change events, no setTimeout polling).

methods.name: onStateChange
methods.signature: (id: string, callback: (oldState: string, newState: string) => void) => () => void
methods.description: Register a callback that fires whenever the specified element's state changes. Returns an unsubscribe function (no cleanup leaks).

methods.name: onEvent
methods.signature: (id: string, event: string, callback: (detail: unknown) => void) => () => void
methods.description: Subscribe to arbitrary probe events scoped to a specific element. Event key format: '{event}:{id}'. Returns an unsubscribe function.

primitives.name: Source Binding
primitives.description: Link UI elements to their API data sources. Intercepts network requests (fetch/XHR/HttpClient/HttpOverrides) and correlates them with elements annotated with data-probe-source. Tracks URL, method, HTTP status, response time, and payload.

methods.name: getSource
methods.description: Get the source binding for an element: URL, method, status code, response time. Returns null if no source tracked.

methods.name: getNetworkLog
methods.description: Return the full network log since probe installation. Each entry includes URL, method, status, timestamp, and matched elementId if correlated.

methods.name: waitForSource
methods.description: Wait until a specific element's source binding is populated (the corresponding network request completes). Web: event-driven via source-update events. RN: waitForRequest() with URL pattern matching.

primitives.name: Layout Metrics
primitives.description: Provide position, size, visibility, scroll position, and render timing metrics from the rendering engine. Uses platform-native APIs (getBoundingClientRect + IntersectionObserver web, RenderBox flutter, UIView.frame iOS, SemanticsNode.boundsInWindow Android, UIElement.RenderSize Windows).

methods.name: getLayout
methods.description: Get current layout metrics: x, y, width, height, visible flag, render time, scroll offsets.

methods.name: getOverlaps
methods.signature: () => Array<{ a: string; b: string; overlapArea: number }>
methods.description: Detect all pairs of overlapping probe elements in the current viewport by computing bounding rect intersection areas. Web: O(n²) comparison in LayoutTracker.

methods.name: getScrollPosition
methods.description: Get the scroll position of a scrollable probe element in pixels.

primitives.name: Action Dispatch
primitives.description: Perform semantic UI operations using probe IDs with smart 5-point pre-checks (exists, visible, enabled, not busy, option exists for select). Every action dispatches real platform events. After each action, automatically verifies linkage targets if declared on the element.
pre_check_sequence: 1. Element exists in registry → NOT_FOUND if missing
4. state.current not in ['loading', 'submitting'] → BUSY if busy
5. For select only: value in data.options → OPTION_NOT_FOUND if missing

methods.name: click
methods.description: Click an element. Web: element.click(). Flutter: WidgetTester tap. iOS: UIControl.sendActions / accessibilityActivate. Android: performClick via ActionPerformer.

methods.name: doubleClick
methods.description: Double-click an element. Web: dispatches dblclick MouseEvent with bubbles:true.

methods.name: rightClick
methods.description: Right-click an element. Web: dispatches contextmenu MouseEvent with button:2.

methods.name: hover
methods.description: Hover over element. Web: dispatches mouseenter then mouseover (both with bubbles).

methods.name: focus
methods.description: Move keyboard focus. Web: element.focus().

methods.name: type
methods.description: Type text character-by-character. Web: dispatches keydown/keypress/input/keyup per char. For automated form filling; use fill() for atomic replacement.

methods.name: fill
methods.description: Set input value atomically. Web: uses native HTMLInputElement.prototype value setter to work with React controlled components, then dispatches input+change events.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Clear an input element. Implemented as fill(id, '').

methods.name: select
methods.description: Select an option from a selector element. Validates option exists against data.options before dispatching.

methods.name: check
methods.description: Set checked state of a checkbox or radio. Web: sets element.checked then dispatches change+input.

methods.name: scrollTo
methods.signature: (id: string, position: { top?: number; left?: number }) => Promise<void>
methods.description: Scroll element to absolute position. Web: element.scrollTo({ behavior: 'instant' }).

methods.name: scrollToBottom
methods.description: Scroll element to maximum scrollTop (element.scrollTop = element.scrollHeight).

methods.name: scrollIntoView
methods.description: Scroll viewport to center element. Web: element.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'center' }).

methods.name: drag
methods.description: Drag-and-drop. Web: dispatches dragstart → dragover → drop → dragend with clientX/Y from getBoundingClientRect. Pre-checks both source AND target elements.

methods.name: pressShortcut
methods.description: Press a keyboard shortcut string (e.g. 'Ctrl+S', 'Meta+Shift+Z'). Web: dispatches to document. Parses ctrl/shift/alt/meta from '+' delimited parts.
methods.note: No element pre-checks — dispatched to document, not a specific element

methods.name: navigate
methods.description: Navigate to a route. Web: history.pushState({}, '', route) then dispatchEvent(new PopStateEvent('popstate')).

auxiliary.name: Platform Context
auxiliary.description: Manage platform, device, and viewport context. Provides 11 built-in device presets and runAcrossDevices() for matrix testing across multiple device profiles with a single test function.

methods.name: setPlatformContext
methods.description: Set the full platform context (platform, device profile, viewport size, input mode).

methods.name: getPlatformContext
methods.description: Get the current platform context. Web: auto-detects from navigator.userAgent + window.innerWidth if not explicitly set.

methods.name: setDevice
methods.description: Apply a named device preset from spec/devices.json (e.g. 'iphone-15-pro', 'pixel-8'). Throws if preset unknown. Available: iphone-se, iphone-15-pro, ipad-air, ipad-pro-12, pixel-8, galaxy-s24, galaxy-tab-s9, galaxy-fold, macbook-air-13, desktop-1080p, desktop-1440p.

methods.name: runAcrossDevices
methods.signature: (devices: Array<ViewportPreset | string>, test: (probe: UITestProbe) => Promise<void>) => Promise<Record<string, TestResult>>
methods.description: Run the same test function across multiple device profiles sequentially. Catches errors per device. Returns { deviceName: { device, passed, duration, error? } }.

auxiliary.name: Hierarchy
auxiliary.description: Navigate the parent-children tree of probe elements. Enables ancestor chain walking for effective visibility checks — child visible=true but parent hidden means effectively invisible.

methods.name: queryChildren
methods.description: Get direct child elements by reading element.children[] probe IDs and resolving from registry.

methods.name: queryDescendants
methods.description: Get all descendants via DFS. Web: recursive collect() calling queryChildren().

methods.name: queryParent
methods.description: Get the parent element by reading element.parent probe ID and resolving from registry.

methods.name: getAncestorChain
methods.description: Get the full ancestor chain from root to the specified element (inclusive). Array ordered root-first. Web: walks parent links until null, unshifts each to front.

methods.name: isEffectivelyVisible
methods.description: Returns false if any ancestor has layout.visible === false. Web: getAncestorChain().every(el => el.layout.visible). iOS: walks UIView parent chain. Windows WPF: walks VisualTreeHelper parent chain.

auxiliary.name: Composite Operations
auxiliary.description: Higher-level operations that compose multiple primitives: snapshot/diff for regression testing, verifyLinkage for end-to-end data flow verification, and actAndWait for action+wait+verify in a single call.

methods.name: snapshot
methods.description: Capture a deep clone of all registered elements and platform context. Web: uses structuredClone with JSON.parse/stringify fallback.
methods.return_type: { timestamp: number; elements: Record<string, ProbeElement>; platformContext: PlatformContext }

methods.name: diff
methods.description: Compare two snapshots and return field-level diffs. Compares state, data, source, layout, linkage fields via JSON.stringify. Also detects added/removed elements via _existence field.
methods.return_type: Array<{ elementId: string; field: string; before: unknown; after: unknown }>

methods.name: verifyLinkage
methods.description: Verify all declared linkage targets respond after an action. Web: waits up to 5000ms per target via state-change events, categorizes direct vs chained effects, tracks API calls for api-type paths.
methods.return_type: { trigger, action, directEffects: [{target, effect, result, duration}], chainedEffects: [{target, effect, through, result, duration}], apiCalls: [{url, method, status, responseTime}] }

methods.name: actAndWait
methods.signature: (id: string, action: string, waitFor: { target?: string; state?: string; timeout?: number }) => Promise<ActAndWaitResult>
methods.description: Dispatch action + wait for target state + auto-verify linkage in one call. Action string format: 'click', 'fill:value', 'select:option'. Returns actionDuration, waitDuration, targetState, linkageResults.

values.description: Table / DataGrid / List / Tree. Type-specific data extensions: sort, filter, selectedRows.
Image / Video / Audio / Canvas. Type-specific data extensions: currentTime, duration, readyState, paused, networkState.
Page-level container. State 'loaded' is the signal for waitForPageReady().
Dialog / Drawer / Popover / Tooltip. state.isOpen tracks open state.

probe_types.platform_divergence: Flutter, iOS, Android SDKs use a simplified 7-type enum (control/display/input/navigation/container/media/feedback) — known divergence from the spec. Windows C# (windows-core) matches all 9 spec types.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Semantic identifier (e.g. 'order-list'). Unique within the probe registry.

attributes.name: type
attributes.description: Widget category (9 values). Determines which data sub-fields are relevant.

attributes.name: accessibility
attributes.description: From ARIA attributes (web) or platform accessibility APIs.

attributes.name: state
attributes.type: { current: string, previous?: string, timestamp: number, isOpen?: boolean, validationErrors?: Array<{field,message}> }
attributes.description: Real-time element state. Common values: loading, loaded, error, empty, disabled, submitting.

attributes.name: data
attributes.type: { value?, options?, rows?, columns?, sort?, filter?, selectedRows?, currentTime?, duration?, readyState?, paused?, networkState? }

attributes.name: source
attributes.type: { url: string, method: string, status?: number, responseTime?: number, payload?: unknown }
attributes.description: API data source binding. Populated by SourceTracker after network request.

attributes.name: linkage
attributes.description: Declared inter-element linkages for automatic verification.

attributes.name: layout
attributes.type: { x, y, width, height, visible: boolean, renderTime?, scrollTop?, scrollLeft? }
attributes.description: Geometric and visibility metrics from the rendering engine.

attributes.name: shortcuts
attributes.type: Array<{ key: string, action: string, platform?: string }>

attributes.name: eventBindings
attributes.description: DOM event types bound to element (e.g. ['click','change']).

values.description: Pure frontend state propagation. No network call. Test only validates state changes.
Through an API call. Test validates API call + response + data mapping.
Derived calculation (e.g. 'quantity * unitPrice'). Test validates formula correctness.
Through state management layer (Redux/Pinia/Bloc). Test validates store action dispatch.
Through a route change. Test validates URL change + target page loaded.
Through an intermediate element (A → B → C). Produces chainedEffects in LinkageResult.

annotation_formats.react_native: const props = useProbe({ id: 'order-list', type: 'data-container' }) then <FlatList {...props} />

flutter.annotation_mechanism: ProbeWidget wrapper with Semantics metadata encoding

android.injection_mechanism: ComposeTestRule extension wiring rootNodeProvider to semantics tree

known_gaps: .NET not available on author's machine for testing

react_native.annotation_mechanism: useProbe() hook / withProbe() HOC — serializes into testID + accessibilityHint JSON
react_native.injection_mechanism: Detox bridge / executeScript for RN probe bridge
